behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is key-derivation determinism/collision logic inside one boundary (key generator), not a cross-module handoff requirement. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text only asserts mapping properties of parameters->key, with no module interaction requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for key generator: same normalized params always produce same key; many distinct param sets produce distinct keys (including ordering/encoding edge cases).

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior is rule lookup/calculation logic for category->TTL, testable in one function/object boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text specifies configured mapping correctness, not config-loading pipeline or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test TTL calculator with table-driven categories and freshness config; assert exact durations, unknown-category fallback, and boundary values.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU." Slot B: decisive signal = handoff (memory-pressure source -> strategy selector -> cache manager). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, you can miss a real wiring bug where threshold events are produced but strategy never updates in the live cache module. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI/ML output, no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text does not require OS-level memory telemetry; a controlled in-process memory-pressure provider is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring memory-pressure monitor module to cache manager with config threshold; drive usage below/above threshold and assert active policy transitions LRU->LFU only at configured boundary.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries." Slot B: decisive signal = real-infra (real PostgreSQL write/notification semantics). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, faked DB/change events can miss trigger/listener/transaction-commit timing failures, so invalidation appears to work only in mocks. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly anchors invalidation to real PostgreSQL writes.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL instance: seed cache entries, execute real table write(s), wait for invalidation mechanism, assert all matching keys are removed and non-matching keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is core policy algorithm correctness (recency/frequency bookkeeping and victim choice) within local cache/policy objects. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on eviction rule outcomes, not cross-module orchestration or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for LRU and LFU policy objects: construct access/update sequences to create unique eviction candidates, fill cache, trigger insert-over-capacity, assert exact key evicted under each policy.
